text: mechanism had locked it open creaked, and the whole thing swung back up, ladder folding into place on its own . She stared at the sealed entry for a few seconds before she stood and dusted her hands off. She’d deal with that problem soon enough. Turning, she studied the room. The slanted walls — from which protruded the tips of countless nails — were lined with wooden beams running from one end to another. The floor was also wood, constructed with close-fitting boards that lacked the impossible polish of the ones downstairs . The room looked…old. Unlike the rest of the house, there was no gleam of cleanliness up here, no coat of paint to hide the age. The musty smell of rotted wood filled the air. This room had been neglected for a long, long time . Stacks of gray boxes stood halfway between the hatch and the window. Nearby was a small, square table with books piled atop it. The chair in front of the table matched the one in the main room downstairs . Lara walked to the table and picked up one of the books, brushing the thick layer of dust off the cover. Between the discoloration and the cracks in the material, she couldn’t tell what it had depicted. The pages inside were stained yellow, crinkly and brittle, but they were intact. She gently ran a finger along the first line of words. Paper was a rare thing amongst humans, complete books even more so . Setting it aside, she leaned down to look at another book. This one had a larger, thinner cover, which depicted a car in full repair. Glancing through the pages, she saw dozens more cars, in more colors and shapes than she’d thought possible. The closest she’d seen were the rusted-out remains of a few old cars some people had used to build their dwellings back home . Her attention shifted to the case of shelves beside the window. It was like the one in her shack, though this was larger and filled with jars instead of trinkets. Moving to it, she lifted one of the jars, brushing away dust and cobwebs. She wiped her hand clean on her skirt . “And I just washed it, too,” she muttered . The jar was heavy, and something dark sloshed inside, but the dirt caked on the glass was too thick to see through. Gripping the lid, she twisted her hand. The metal ring resisted for a moment before the rust gave way. The metal plate beneath proved more difficult. She pressed her fingers on its edge, wincing as she pried at it. Finally, it released its suction, coming loose with a pop . The foulest odor she’d ever smelled hit Lara directly in the face. Gagging, she nearly dropped the jar, extending her arm to keep it as far away as possible. She glanced at the unidentifiable black sludge inside through slitted, tear- filled eyes . “Ugh!” Slapping the seal back on top, she quickly screwed the ring back on and returned the jar to its place. “What was that ?” She reached up to wipe her face, but caught another whiff of the smell on her fingers. Nose crinkling, she rubbed her hands roughly on her skirt . “Yeah. Gonna need to wash these again .” Strange how her attitude had changed overnight; she’d rarely washed her clothes, before. It only made them wear out faster, and she knew they’d be filthy the next day anyway, after hours spent in heat and dirt. But feeling soft cloth against clean skin…it was going to be difficult to let that go. She’d have to enjoy it while she could . It was only a matter of time before Ronin lost interest in her . The room, despite the thick dust, was comfortable. Though it was far from perfect, it wasn’t falling apart; this place had been lived in. It reminded her of her little shack. Long enough up here, and she might forget she was in the Bot District at all . She used the end of her skirt to wipe away the grime on the window. The grassy field stretched out below, and from this height the pond near its center was visible. Was this how humans used to live? In quiet comfort ? As Lara turned, her foot bumped into something. She glanced down at a pile of tattered blankets. Crouching, she lifted them up; the top blanket crumbled to fibers at her touch. She tossed the bunch aside, revealing an assortment of items. The clothes were in the same ratty condition as the blankets, and looked to have belonged to a man. They were wrapped around a few more books . But it was the strange device with the wire trailing off it that caught her attention first. Rectangular, it was small enough to fit comfortably in her hand, and had a few buttons on the front and sides. Its glass face was cracked. The wire split after about two feet, ending in a pair of small, strangely- shaped buds . Placing it atop the blankets, she looked through the books. The first few were thin and flimsy, like the one with the car pictures, and she nearly dropped one when she opened it . “Oh.” She’d turned to a page with a large photo of a naked woman, hands lifting her oversized breasts. Many of the other pages displayed similar images — women in various poses, either naked or damned near it . They reminded her of the synths at Kitty’s. Their bodies were full and lush, their skin flawless, without a hair out of place — and most without hair on their pussies. Some of them had unnaturally large breasts, bigger than any Lara had seen. There was color on their lips and around their eyes — deep reds and pinks, shades of blue, green, purple, and black. With their perfect figures and faces, she would
